// ============================================
// Photography Pixel Hub — Prisma Schema
// ============================================

generator client {
  provider = "prisma-client-js"
}

datasource db {
  provider = "postgresql"
}

// ============================================
// Enums
// ============================================

enum UserRole {
  OWNER
  ADMIN
  EDITOR
  MEDIA_BUYER
  PHOTOGRAPHER
  CLIENT
}

enum ProjectStatus {
  DRAFT
  IN_PROGRESS
  READY
  DOWNLOAD_ENABLED
  COMPLETED
  ARCHIVED
}

enum RetentionPeriod {
  TWENTY_FOUR_HOURS
  FORTY_EIGHT_HOURS
  SEVENTY_TWO_HOURS
  SEVEN_DAYS
  CUSTOM
}

enum VideoStatus {
  UPLOADING
  PROCESSING
  READY
  FAILED
  DELETED
}

enum ActivityType {
  CREATE
  UPDATE
  DELETE
  LOGIN
  LOGOUT
  UPLOAD
  DOWNLOAD
  VIEW
  ARCHIVE
  RESTORE
  RETENTION_CHANGE
  DELIVERY_CREATED
  DELIVERY_UPDATED
  DELIVERY_PUBLISHED
  DELIVERY_DISABLED
  DELIVERY_VIEWED
  DOWNLOAD_STARTED
  DOWNLOAD_COMPLETED
  COMMENT_CREATED
  COMMENT_UPDATED
  COMMENT_RESOLVED
  COMMENT_REOPENED
  COMMENT_ARCHIVED
  VIDEO_APPROVED
}

enum ClientStatus {
  ACTIVE
  ARCHIVED
  BLOCKED
}

enum DeliveryStatus {
  ACTIVE
  EXPIRED
  DISABLED
}

enum CommentStatus {
  OPEN
  RESOLVED
  ARCHIVED
}

enum AuthorType {
  CLIENT
  TEAM
}

enum ModelStatus {
  ACTIVE
  INACTIVE
}

enum PaymentStatus {
  PENDING
  PARTIALLY_PAID
  PAID
}

enum QuoteStatus {
  DRAFT
  SENT
  APPROVED
  REJECTED
  EXPIRED
}

enum InvoiceStatus {
  DRAFT
  SENT
  PARTIALLY_PAID
  PAID
  OVERDUE
  CANCELLED
}

enum PaymentMethod {
  CASH
  BANK_TRANSFER
  CREDIT_CARD
  OTHER
}

enum TeamMemberStatus {
  ACTIVE
  INACTIVE
  ON_LEAVE
}

enum TeamRole {
  OWNER
  ADMIN
  PROJECT_MANAGER
  PHOTOGRAPHER
  VIDEOGRAPHER
  EDITOR
  DESIGNER
  MEDIA_BUYER
  ACCOUNTANT
}

enum ShootStatus {
  SCHEDULED
  CONFIRMED
  IN_PROGRESS
  COMPLETED
  CANCELLED
}

enum TaskStatus {
  TODO
  IN_PROGRESS
  IN_REVIEW
  BLOCKED
  DONE
}

enum TaskPriority {
  LOW
  MEDIUM
  HIGH
  URGENT
}

enum ProjectWorkflowStatus {
  NEW
  PLANNING
  SHOOTING
  EDITING
  REVIEW
  REVISION
  APPROVED
  DELIVERED
  PAID
  COMPLETED
  ARCHIVED
}

enum ProjectEventType {
  PROJECT_CREATED
  PROJECT_UPDATED
  PROJECT_STATUS_CHANGED
  VIDEOS_UPLOADED
  UPLOAD_FAILED
  DELIVERY_CREATED
  DELIVERY_PUBLISHED
  DELIVERY_VIEWED
  DOWNLOAD_STARTED
  DOWNLOAD_COMPLETED
  COMMENT_ADDED
  COMMENT_RESOLVED
  VIDEO_APPROVED
  INVOICE_CREATED
  INVOICE_PAID
  PROJECT_COMPLETED
  WORKFLOW_TRANSITION
  MODEL_ASSIGNED
  MODEL_REMOVED
  MODEL_VIDEOS_UPDATED
  MODEL_PAYMENT_UPDATED
  QUOTE_CREATED
  QUOTE_SENT
  QUOTE_APPROVED
  QUOTE_REJECTED
  INVOICE_SENT
  PAYMENT_RECEIVED
  TEAM_MEMBER_ASSIGNED
  TEAM_MEMBER_REMOVED
  TEAM_ROLE_CHANGED
  SHOOT_CREATED
  SHOOT_UPDATED
  SHOOT_CONFIRMED
  SHOOT_STARTED
  SHOOT_COMPLETED
  SHOOT_CANCELLED
  TASK_CREATED
  TASK_ASSIGNED
  TASK_UPDATED
  TASK_STARTED
  TASK_COMPLETED
  TASK_OVERDUE
}

enum ServiceType {
  VIDEO
  PHOTOGRAPHY
  BOTH
  EDITING
}

enum Priority {
  LOW
  MEDIUM
  HIGH
  URGENT
}

enum NotificationType {
  PROJECT_CREATED
  MODEL_ASSIGNED
  UPLOAD_FINISHED
  CLIENT_COMMENTED
  CLIENT_APPROVED
  CHANGES_REQUESTED
  DOWNLOAD_ENABLED
  DOWNLOAD_COMPLETED
  PROJECT_COMPLETED
  STATUS_CHANGED
}

enum ProjectFileType {
  PREVIEW_VIDEO
  FINAL_VIDEO
  THUMBNAIL
  ASSET
  MUSIC
  LOGO
  DOCUMENT
  INVOICE
  CONTRACT
}

// ============================================
// Models
// ============================================

model User {
  id          String   @id @default(uuid()) @db.Uuid
  email       String   @unique
  name        String
  supabaseUid String?  @unique @db.Uuid
  role        UserRole @default(PHOTOGRAPHER)
  avatar      String?
  createdAt   DateTime @default(now())
  updatedAt   DateTime @updatedAt

  clients      Client[]
  activityLogs ActivityLog[]
  notifications Notification[]

  @@index([email])
  @@index([supabaseUid])
  @@map("users")
}

model Client {
  id         String       @id @default(uuid()) @db.Uuid
  userId     String       @db.Uuid
  clientCode String       @unique
  name       String
  company    String?
  email      String?
  phone       String?
  city        String?
  notes       String?
  status     ClientStatus @default(ACTIVE)
  token      String       @unique
  archivedAt DateTime?
  createdAt  DateTime     @default(now())
  updatedAt  DateTime      @updatedAt

  user      User       @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  projects  Project[]
  downloads Download[]
  views     View[]
  quotes    Quote[]
  invoices  Invoice[]

  @@unique([userId, email])
  @@index([userId])
  @@index([email])
  @@index([token])
  @@index([status])
  @@index([clientCode])
  @@map("clients")
}

model Project {
  id             String         @id @default(uuid()) @db.Uuid
  projectCode    String         @unique
  clientId       String         @db.Uuid
  name           String
  brandName      String?
  serviceType    ServiceType?
  priority       Priority       @default(MEDIUM)
  description    String?
  status         ProjectStatus  @default(DRAFT)
  workflowStatus ProjectWorkflowStatus @default(NEW)
  retentionPeriod RetentionPeriod @default(TWENTY_FOUR_HOURS)
  deadline       DateTime?
  shootingDate   DateTime?
  price          Float?
  token          String         @unique
  archivedAt     DateTime?
  createdAt      DateTime       @default(now())
  updatedAt      DateTime       @updatedAt

  client    Client    @relation(fields: [clientId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  videos    Video[]
  downloads Download[]
  views     View[]
  deliveries Delivery[]
  timelineEvents ProjectTimelineEvent[]
  projectModels ProjectModel[]
  quotes    Quote[]
  invoices  Invoice[]
  projectAssignments ProjectAssignment[]
  shoots    Shoot[]
  tasks     Task[]
  projectFiles ProjectFile[]

  @@index([clientId])
  @@index([status])
  @@index([workflowStatus])
  @@index([token])
  @@index([projectCode])
  @@index([archivedAt])
  @@index([retentionPeriod])
  @@map("projects")
}

model Video {
  id              String       @id @default(uuid()) @db.Uuid
  videoCode       String       @unique
  projectId       String       @db.Uuid
  title           String
  originalFileName String
  storageKey      String       @unique
  storageBucket   String
  mimeType        String
  extension       String
  fileSize        BigInt
  duration        Int?
  width           Int?
  height          Int?
  thumbnailUrl    String?
  streamUrl       String?
  downloadUrl     String?
  status          VideoStatus  @default(UPLOADING)
  uploadedAt      DateTime?
  processedAt     DateTime?
  deletedAt       DateTime?
  createdAt       DateTime     @default(now())
  updatedAt       DateTime     @updatedAt

  project   Project     @relation(fields: [projectId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  downloads Download[]
  views     View[]
  deliveryVideos DeliveryVideo[]
  reviewComments  ReviewComment[]

  @@index([projectId])
  @@index([status])
  @@index([videoCode])
  @@index([deletedAt])
  @@map("videos")
}

model Download {
  id        String   @id @default(uuid()) @db.Uuid
  videoId   String   @db.Uuid
  clientId  String   @db.Uuid
  projectId String   @db.Uuid
  ip        String?
  userAgent String?
  browser   String?
  device    String?
  createdAt DateTime @default(now())
  updatedAt DateTime @updatedAt

  video   Video   @relation(fields: [videoId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  client  Client  @relation(fields: [clientId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  project Project @relation(fields: [projectId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)

  @@index([videoId])
  @@index([clientId])
  @@index([projectId])
  @@map("downloads")
}

model View {
  id        String   @id @default(uuid()) @db.Uuid
  videoId   String   @db.Uuid
  clientId  String   @db.Uuid
  projectId String   @db.Uuid
  ip        String?
  userAgent String?
  duration   Int?
  createdAt DateTime @default(now())
  updatedAt DateTime @updatedAt

  video   Video   @relation(fields: [videoId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  client  Client  @relation(fields: [clientId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  project Project @relation(fields: [projectId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)

  @@index([videoId])
  @@index([clientId])
  @@index([projectId])
  @@map("views")
}

model Delivery {
  id               String         @id @default(uuid()) @db.Uuid
  deliveryCode     String         @unique
  projectId       String         @db.Uuid
  title           String
  slug            String         @unique
  status          DeliveryStatus @default(ACTIVE)
  expiresAt       DateTime?
  downloadEnabled Boolean        @default(true)
  allowStreaming  Boolean        @default(true)
  allowComments   Boolean        @default(false)
  passwordProtected Boolean      @default(false)
  passwordHash    String?
  viewCount       Int            @default(0)
  downloadCount   Int            @default(0)
  lastViewedAt    DateTime?
  createdAt       DateTime       @default(now())
  updatedAt       DateTime       @updatedAt

  project  Project          @relation(fields: [projectId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  videos   DeliveryVideo[]
  reviewComments  ReviewComment[]

  @@index([projectId])
  @@index([status])
  @@index([slug])
  @@index([deliveryCode])
  @@map("deliveries")
}

model DeliveryVideo {
  id         String   @id @default(uuid()) @db.Uuid
  deliveryId String   @db.Uuid
  videoId    String   @db.Uuid
  order      Int      @default(0)
  createdAt  DateTime @default(now())

  delivery Delivery @relation(fields: [deliveryId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  video    Video    @relation(fields: [videoId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)

  @@unique([deliveryId, videoId])
  @@index([deliveryId])
  @@index([videoId])
  @@map("delivery_videos")
}

model ReviewComment {
  id                String        @id @default(uuid()) @db.Uuid
  commentCode       String        @unique
  videoId           String        @db.Uuid
  deliveryId        String        @db.Uuid
  parentId          String?      @db.Uuid
  authorName        String
  authorEmail       String
  authorType        AuthorType    @default(CLIENT)
  message           String
  timestampSeconds  Int
  status            CommentStatus @default(OPEN)
  resolvedAt        DateTime?
  createdAt         DateTime      @default(now())
  updatedAt         DateTime      @updatedAt

  video     Video           @relation(fields: [videoId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  delivery  Delivery        @relation(fields: [deliveryId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  parent    ReviewComment?  @relation("CommentReplies", fields: [parentId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  replies   ReviewComment[] @relation("CommentReplies")

  @@index([videoId])
  @@index([deliveryId])
  @@index([parentId])
  @@index([status])
  @@index([commentCode])
  @@map("review_comments")
}

model ProjectTimelineEvent {
  id          String           @id @default(uuid()) @db.Uuid
  projectId   String           @db.Uuid
  eventType   ProjectEventType
  title       String
  description String?
  metadata    Json?
  actorId     String?          @db.Uuid
  actorName   String
  createdAt   DateTime         @default(now())

  project Project @relation(fields: [projectId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)

  @@index([projectId])
  @@index([eventType])
  @@index([createdAt])
  @@map("project_timeline_events")
}

model Model {
  id        String      @id @default(uuid()) @db.Uuid
  modelCode String      @unique
  fullName  String
  phone     String
  whatsapp  String?
  email     String?
  photo     String?
  status    ModelStatus @default(ACTIVE)
  availability String?
  notes     String?
  createdAt DateTime    @default(now())
  updatedAt DateTime    @updatedAt

  projectModels ProjectModel[]
  shootAssignments ShootAssignment[]

  @@index([status])
  @@index([modelCode])
  @@index([phone])
  @@map("models")
}

model ProjectModel {
  id            String        @id @default(uuid()) @db.Uuid
  projectId     String        @db.Uuid
  modelId       String        @db.Uuid
  videosCount   Int           @default(1)
  pricePerVideo Float         @default(0)
  totalAmount   Float         @default(0)
  paymentStatus PaymentStatus @default(PENDING)
  script        String?
  notes         String?
  createdAt     DateTime      @default(now())
  updatedAt     DateTime      @updatedAt

  project Project @relation(fields: [projectId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  model   Model   @relation(fields: [modelId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)

  @@unique([projectId, modelId])
  @@index([projectId])
  @@index([modelId])
  @@index([paymentStatus])
  @@map("project_models")
}

model Quote {
  id         String      @id @default(uuid()) @db.Uuid
  quoteCode  String      @unique
  clientId   String      @db.Uuid
  projectId  String?     @db.Uuid
  status     QuoteStatus @default(DRAFT)
  subtotal   Float       @default(0)
  discount   Float       @default(0)
  tax        Float       @default(0)
  total      Float       @default(0)
  notes      String?
  validUntil DateTime?
  createdAt  DateTime    @default(now())
  updatedAt  DateTime    @updatedAt

  client  Client      @relation(fields: [clientId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  project Project?    @relation(fields: [projectId], references: [id], onDelete: SetNull)
  items   QuoteItem[]
  invoice Invoice?

  @@index([clientId])
  @@index([projectId])
  @@index([status])
  @@index([quoteCode])
  @@map("quotes")
}

model QuoteItem {
  id          String @id @default(uuid()) @db.Uuid
  quoteId     String @db.Uuid
  description String
  quantity    Int    @default(1)
  unitPrice   Float  @default(0)
  total       Float  @default(0)
  order       Int    @default(0)
  createdAt   DateTime @default(now())

  quote Quote @relation(fields: [quoteId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)

  @@index([quoteId])
  @@map("quote_items")
}

model Invoice {
  id              String        @id @default(uuid()) @db.Uuid
  invoiceCode     String        @unique
  clientId        String        @db.Uuid
  projectId       String?       @db.Uuid
  quoteId         String?       @db.Uuid @unique
  status          InvoiceStatus @default(DRAFT)
  subtotal        Float         @default(0)
  discount        Float         @default(0)
  tax             Float         @default(0)
  total           Float         @default(0)
  paidAmount      Float         @default(0)
  remainingAmount Float         @default(0)
  issueDate       DateTime      @default(now())
  dueDate         DateTime?
  notes           String?
  createdAt       DateTime      @default(now())
  updatedAt       DateTime      @updatedAt

  client   Client        @relation(fields: [clientId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  project  Project?      @relation(fields: [projectId], references: [id], onDelete: SetNull)
  quote    Quote?        @relation(fields: [quoteId], references: [id], onDelete: SetNull)
  items    InvoiceItem[]
  payments Payment[]

  @@index([clientId])
  @@index([projectId])
  @@index([quoteId])
  @@index([status])
  @@index([invoiceCode])
  @@map("invoices")
}

model InvoiceItem {
  id          String @id @default(uuid()) @db.Uuid
  invoiceId   String @db.Uuid
  description String
  quantity    Int    @default(1)
  unitPrice   Float  @default(0)
  total       Float  @default(0)
  order       Int    @default(0)
  createdAt   DateTime @default(now())

  invoice Invoice @relation(fields: [invoiceId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)

  @@index([invoiceId])
  @@map("invoice_items")
}

model Payment {
  id            String        @id @default(uuid()) @db.Uuid
  paymentCode   String        @unique
  invoiceId     String        @db.Uuid
  amount        Float
  paymentMethod PaymentMethod @default(CASH)
  reference     String?
  notes         String?
  paidAt        DateTime      @default(now())
  createdAt     DateTime      @default(now())

  invoice Invoice @relation(fields: [invoiceId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)

  @@index([invoiceId])
  @@index([paymentCode])
  @@map("payments")
}

model TeamMember {
  id            String           @id @default(uuid()) @db.Uuid
  employeeCode  String           @unique
  fullName      String
  email         String           @unique
  phone         String
  photo         String?
  role          TeamRole         @default(PHOTOGRAPHER)
  status        TeamMemberStatus @default(ACTIVE)
  joinDate      DateTime         @default(now())
  notes         String?
  createdAt     DateTime         @default(now())
  updatedAt     DateTime         @updatedAt

  projectAssignments ProjectAssignment[]
  shootAssignments   ShootAssignment[]
  tasks              Task[]
  taskComments       TaskComment[]

  @@index([status])
  @@index([role])
  @@index([employeeCode])
  @@index([email])
  @@map("team_members")
}

model ProjectAssignment {
  id            String   @id @default(uuid()) @db.Uuid
  projectId     String   @db.Uuid
  teamMemberId String   @db.Uuid
  role          TeamRole @default(PHOTOGRAPHER)
  assignedAt    DateTime @default(now())
  completedAt   DateTime?
  notes         String?
  createdAt     DateTime @default(now())
  updatedAt     DateTime @updatedAt

  project    Project     @relation(fields: [projectId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  teamMember TeamMember  @relation(fields: [teamMemberId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)

  @@unique([projectId, teamMemberId])
  @@index([projectId])
  @@index([teamMemberId])
  @@index([role])
  @@map("project_assignments")
}

model Shoot {
  id          String      @id @default(uuid()) @db.Uuid
  shootCode   String      @unique
  projectId   String      @db.Uuid
  title       String
  description String?
  location    String?
  date        DateTime
  startTime   DateTime
  endTime     DateTime
  status      ShootStatus @default(SCHEDULED)
  notes       String?
  createdAt   DateTime    @default(now())
  updatedAt   DateTime    @updatedAt

  project    Project            @relation(fields: [projectId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  assignments ShootAssignment[]
  tasks      Task[]

  @@index([projectId])
  @@index([status])
  @@index([date])
  @@index([shootCode])
  @@map("shoots")
}

model ShootAssignment {
  id            String    @id @default(uuid()) @db.Uuid
  shootId       String    @db.Uuid
  teamMemberId  String?   @db.Uuid
  modelId       String?   @db.Uuid
  role          String
  createdAt     DateTime  @default(now())

  shoot       Shoot       @relation(fields: [shootId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  teamMember  TeamMember? @relation(fields: [teamMemberId], references: [id], onDelete: SetNull)
  model       Model?      @relation(fields: [modelId], references: [id], onDelete: SetNull)

  @@index([shootId])
  @@index([teamMemberId])
  @@index([modelId])
  @@map("shoot_assignments")
}

model Task {
  id             String       @id @default(uuid()) @db.Uuid
  taskCode       String       @unique
  projectId      String       @db.Uuid
  shootId        String?      @db.Uuid
  parentTaskId   String?      @db.Uuid
  title          String
  description    String?
  status         TaskStatus   @default(TODO)
  priority       TaskPriority @default(MEDIUM)
  assignedTo     String?      @db.Uuid
  estimatedHours Float        @default(0)
  actualHours    Float        @default(0)
  startDate      DateTime?
  dueDate        DateTime
  completedAt    DateTime?
  progress       Int          @default(0)
  order          Int          @default(0)
  createdAt      DateTime     @default(now())
  updatedAt      DateTime     @updatedAt

  project      Project          @relation(fields: [projectId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  shoot        Shoot?           @relation(fields: [shootId], references: [id], onDelete: SetNull)
  parentTask   Task?            @relation("TaskSubtasks", fields: [parentTaskId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  subtasks     Task[]           @relation("TaskSubtasks")
  assignee     TeamMember?      @relation(fields: [assignedTo], references: [id], onDelete: SetNull)
  checklists   TaskChecklist[]
  comments     TaskComment[]
  attachments  TaskAttachment[]

  @@index([projectId])
  @@index([shootId])
  @@index([parentTaskId])
  @@index([assignedTo])
  @@index([status])
  @@index([priority])
  @@index([taskCode])
  @@index([dueDate])
  @@map("tasks")
}

model TaskChecklist {
  id        String   @id @default(uuid()) @db.Uuid
  taskId    String   @db.Uuid
  title     String
  completed Boolean  @default(false)
  order     Int      @default(0)
  createdAt DateTime @default(now())

  task Task @relation(fields: [taskId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)

  @@index([taskId])
  @@map("task_checklists")
}

model TaskComment {
  id        String   @id @default(uuid()) @db.Uuid
  taskId    String   @db.Uuid
  authorId  String   @db.Uuid
  content   String
  createdAt DateTime @default(now())

  task  Task       @relation(fields: [taskId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  author TeamMember @relation(fields: [authorId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)

  @@index([taskId])
  @@index([authorId])
  @@map("task_comments")
}

model TaskAttachment {
  id         String   @id @default(uuid()) @db.Uuid
  taskId     String   @db.Uuid
  fileName   String
  storageKey String
  mime       String
  size       BigInt
  createdAt  DateTime @default(now())

  task Task @relation(fields: [taskId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)

  @@index([taskId])
  @@map("task_attachments")
}

model ActivityLog {
  id        String       @id @default(uuid()) @db.Uuid
  userId    String       @db.Uuid
  type      ActivityType
  entity    String
  entityId   String
  metadata   Json?
  createdAt DateTime     @default(now())
  updatedAt DateTime     @updatedAt

  user User @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)

  @@index([userId])
  @@index([type])
  @@index([entity, entityId])
  @@map("activity_logs")
}

model Notification {
  id        String           @id @default(uuid()) @db.Uuid
  userId    String           @db.Uuid
  type      NotificationType
  title     String
  message   String
  entity    String?
  entityId  String?
  readAt    DateTime?
  createdAt DateTime         @default(now())

  user User @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)

  @@index([userId, readAt])
  @@index([createdAt])
  @@map("notifications")
}

model ProjectFile {
  id          String          @id @default(uuid()) @db.Uuid
  projectId   String          @db.Uuid
  fileType    ProjectFileType
  storageKey  String
  fileName    String
  fileSize    Int             @default(0)
  mimeType    String          @default("")
  uploadedBy  String?         @db.Uuid
  createdAt   DateTime        @default(now())

  project Project @relation(fields: [projectId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)

  @@index([projectId])
  @@index([fileType])
  @@map("project_files")
}

### 5. Review Studio — Full Feature Inventory

The primary component is **`ReviewStudio`** in `src/components/review/review-studio.tsx` (~700 lines):

**Features implemented**:
- **Phone mockup**: `PhoneFrame` with titanium border gradient, Dynamic Island, speaker grill, glass reflections, gesture bar — 9:16 aspect ratio
- **Status bar**: `StatusBar` with live time, signal bars, WiFi icon, battery indicator
- **Social platform overlays**: `InstagramOverlay`, `TikTokOverlay`, `YouTubeOverlay`, `FacebookOverlay` — segmented control switcher
- **Safe zones overlay**: `SafeZoneOverlay` with red (platform UI), yellow (danger), green (safe) translucent overlays — configurable per platform (Instagram 10%/28%/12%, TikTok 6%/30%/14%, etc.)
- **Play/Pause**: Tap-to-toggle with play icon overlay when paused
- **Fullscreen**: Via `requestFullscreen()` API
- **Volume**: Mute/unmute toggle + volume slider
- **Playback speed**: Menu showing 0.5x, 1x, 1.25x, 1.5x, 2x
- **Seek bar**: With timeline markers (comments as dots) and hover tooltips
- **Comments system**: Full CRUD — create at timestamp, reply, resolve, archive, filter, search
- **Approval workflow**: Approve button → transitions to `APPROVED`; Revision button → opens `RevisionDialog` (transitions to `REVISION`)
- **Download**: For `FINAL_VIDEO` when workflow is `DELIVERED`/`COMPLETED`
- **Double-tap heart**: Animated heart at tap position
- **Swipe navigation**: `useSwipeNavigation` hook for vertical swipe between videos
- **Hold-to-speed**: `useHoldToSpeed` — hold video for 2x playback
- **Auto-hide UI**: `useAutoHideUI` — hides controls during playback
- **Screenshot mode**: Chromeless view for clean screenshots
- **Presentation mode**: Full-screen chromeless for presentations
- **Draft comments**: Auto-save via `useDraftComment` hook
- **Keyboard shortcuts**: Via `useReviewKeyboard` hook
- **Desktop timeline**: Separate TimelineBar under the phone on desktop
- **Video selector**: Bottom tab bar for switching between multiple videos
- **Comment form**: Floating inline form at timestamp with name/email fields

**ReviewPlayer** (`src/components/review/review-player.tsx`): A simpler alternative for the public delivery portal — standard video player + comments panel + approve button.

### 6. API Routes

| Route | Method | Auth | Purpose |
|-------|--------|------|---------|
| `/api/health` | GET | No | Health check |
| `/api/upload` | POST | Yes | File upload endpoint |
| `/api/webhooks` | POST | TBD | Webhook receiver |

### 7. Auth Pattern — Supabase Auth + Prisma

**Architecture**: Dual-source auth:
1. **Supabase Auth** manages credentials, sessions, and session cookies
2. **Prisma `User` table** stores profile (name, role, avatar) linked via `supabaseUid`

**Flow**:
- **Session reads** (`src/lib/auth/session.ts`): `getCurrentUser()` reads supabase session → looks up Prisma user by `supabaseUid` → returns `SessionUser` or null
- **Role guards**: `requireUser()`, `requireRole(...roles)` — throws `UnauthorizedError`/`ForbiddenError`
- **RBAC** (`src/lib/auth/rbac.ts`): `requireAuth()`, `requireAdmin()`, `requireManagement()` — used in Server Actions
- **Permissions** (`src/lib/auth/permissions.ts`): `requireOwner()`, `requireAdmin()`, `requireEditor()`, `requireMediaBuyer()`, `requirePhotographer()` — fine-grained guards

**Middleware (proxy.ts)**:
- Next.js 16 `proxy()` function (replaces middleware)
- Refreshes Supabase session
- Protects `/dashboard/*` routes → redirects to `/login`
- Redirects authenticated users away from `/login`
- Sets security headers: CSP, HSTS, X-Frame-Options, etc.
- Matcher: `/((?!_next/static|_next/image|favicon.ico|logo|icons|images).*)`

**Public/Token-based access** exists:
- `/delivery/[slug]` — No auth required. Optional password protection via `delivery.passwordHash`
- `/p/[token]` — Token-based access via `project.token`
- The `accessDeliveryAction` server action is public (no `getCurrentUser()` call)

**Auth actions** (`src/actions/auth/`): `login.ts`, `logout.ts`, `register.ts`, `update-profile.ts`
**Login flow**: `loginAction` → `authService.login()` → `supabase.auth.signInWithPassword()` → looks up Prisma user → returns `AuthenticatedUser`

**Session provider** (`src/providers/session-provider.tsx`): React Context with `user`, `status` (`loading`/`authenticated`/`unauthenticated`), `setUser`, `setStatus`

### 8. Storage Pattern — Cloudflare R2 (via AWS SDK) + Signed URLs

**Provider**: Cloudflare R2 (S3-compatible) — `src/lib/storage/cloudflare-r2.provider.ts`
- Uses `@aws-sdk/client-s3` and `@aws-sdk/s3-request-presigner`
- Lazy-initialized S3 client
- Operations: `upload`, `replace`, `delete`, `moveFile`, `copyFile`, `listFiles`, `exists`, `getMetadata`, `getSignedUrl`, `getDownloadUrl`, `getStreamingUrl`, `generateUploadUrl`

**Storage service** (`src/lib/storage/storage.service.ts`): Singleton facade — the only storage entry point

**Signed URL expiration**:
- Download: 1 hour (3600s)
- Streaming: 2 hours (7200s)
- Upload (presigned): 10 minutes (600s)

# ============================================
# Photography Pixel Hub – Environment Variables
# ============================================
# Copy this file to .env.local and fill in your values.
# For Vercel deployment, add these in the Vercel dashboard.

# ── App ───────────────────────────────────
NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_NAME="Photography Pixel Hub"
NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL=http://localhost:3000

# ── Database (Supabase Postgres) ──────────
# Connect via the shared transaction-mode pooler (IPv4-only)
DATABASE_URL="postgresql://postgres.[project-ref]:[password]@aws-0-[region].pooler.supabase.com:6543/postgres?pgbouncer=true"
# Connect via the shared session-mode pooler (used for migrations)
DIRECT_URL="postgresql://postgres.[project-ref]:[password]@aws-0-[region].pooler.supabase.com:5432/postgres"

# ── Supabase (Auth only — NOT Storage) ───
# Get from: Supabase Dashboard → Settings → API
NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL=https://[project-ref].supabase.co
NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY=[supabase-anon-key]
SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY=[supabase-service-role-key]

# ── Cloudflare R2 (Storage) ───────────────
# Get from: Cloudflare Dashboard → R2 → Overview (Account ID)
#           Cloudflare Dashboard → R2 → Manage API Tokens
R2_ACCOUNT_ID=YOUR_ACCOUNT_ID
R2_ACCESS_KEY_ID=YOUR_ACCESS_KEY

## Photography Pixel Hub — Complete Codebase Analysis

### 1. Project Structure

```
D:\Photography-Pixel-Hub\
├── prisma/                  # Prisma ORM — schema + migrations
│   └── schema.prisma        # Single file, massive schema (40+ models)
├── public/                  # Static assets — logos, icons, favicons
│   └── icons/, images/, logo/
├── scripts/                 # Seed, deploy, debug scripts
├── src/
│   ├── actions/             # 104 Server Actions (see below)
│   ├── app/                 # Next.js App Router
│   │   ├── (auth)/          # Route group: login
│   │   ├── api/             # 3 API routes
│   │   ├── dashboard/       # 20+ protected dashboard pages
│   │   ├── delivery/[slug]/ # Public delivery portal
│   │   └── p/[token]/       # Public token-based gallery
│   ├── components/          # 20 component directories (shadcn/ui + custom)
│   ├── config/              # branding.ts
│   ├── hooks/               # 10 custom hooks
│   ├── lib/                 # Core libraries (auth, storage, supabase, utils, etc.)
│   ├── providers/            # 3 React providers (query, session, theme)
│   ├── repositories/         # 17 data-access layer files
│   ├── services/             # 21 business-logic service files
│   └── types/               # 15 TypeScript type definition files
├── proxy.ts                 # Next.js 16 proxy (middleware replacement)
├── uploads/                 # Local upload directory (contains only .gitkeep)
└── docs/, mem-log/          # Documentation and memory logs
```

### 2. Routing (Next.js App Router — ALL Existing Routes)

**Root layout** (`src/app/layout.tsx`):
- RTL Arabic (`lang="ar" dir="rtl"`), Cairo + Inter fonts
- Wraps all children in `ThemeProvider` (dark mode default) → `SessionProvider` (reads user from supabase server) → `QueryProvider` (tanstack/react-query)
- `Toaster` from sonner for notifications

**26 Pages found**:

| Route | File | Access |
|-------|------|--------|
| `/` | `src/app/page.tsx` | Public — redirects to `/dashboard` if logged in, otherwise `/login` |
| `/login` | `src/app/(auth)/login/page.tsx` | Public (redirected away if authenticated) |
| `/dashboard` | `src/app/dashboard/page.tsx` | Protected (team dashboard home) |

| `/dashboard/projects/[id]/review` | `src/app/dashboard/projects/[id]/review/page.tsx` | Protected |
| `/dashboard/projects/[id]/videos` | `src/app/dashboard/projects/[id]/videos/page.tsx` | Protected |

**3 API Routes**:

|-------|------|---------|

### 3. Database Schema (Prisma — Full Summary)

**File**: `D:\Photography-Pixel-Hub\prisma\schema.prisma`  
**Provider**: PostgreSQL (via Supabase)  
**Enums (24 total)**: UserRole, ProjectStatus, RetentionPeriod, VideoStatus, ActivityType, ClientStatus, DeliveryStatus, CommentStatus, AuthorType, ModelStatus, PaymentStatus, QuoteStatus, InvoiceStatus, PaymentMethod, TeamMemberStatus, TeamRole, ShootStatus, TaskStatus, TaskPriority, ProjectWorkflowStatus, ProjectEventType, ServiceType, Priority, NotificationType, ProjectFileType

**Key Models (40 total)**:

| Model | Map | Key Fields |
|-------|-----|-----------|
| **User** | `users` | id (UUID), email (unique), supabaseUid (unique UUID), role (UserRole), avatar |
| **Client** | `clients` | id, clientCode (unique), **token (unique)**, status, userId FK → User |
| **Project** | `projects` | id, projectCode (unique), **token (unique)**, clientId FK, status, workflowStatus, retentionPeriod, deadline |
| **Video** | `videos` | id, videoCode (unique), projectId FK, storageKey (unique), storageBucket, mimeType, extension, fileSize (BigInt), duration, width, height, thumbnailUrl, streamUrl, downloadUrl, status |
| **Download** | `downloads` | id, videoId FK, clientId FK, projectId FK, ip, userAgent, browser, device |
| **View** | `views` | id, videoId FK, clientId FK, projectId FK, ip, userAgent, duration |
| **Delivery** | `deliveries` | id, deliveryCode (unique), projectId FK, **slug (unique)**, status, expiresAt, downloadEnabled, allowStreaming, allowComments, passwordProtected, passwordHash, viewCount, downloadCount |
| **DeliveryVideo** | `delivery_videos` | id, deliveryId FK, videoId FK, order — join table |
| **ReviewComment** | `review_comments` | id, commentCode (unique), videoId FK, deliveryId FK, parentId (self-ref FK), authorName, authorEmail, authorType, message, timestampSeconds, status |
| **ProjectTimelineEvent** | `project_timeline_events` | id, projectId FK, eventType (ProjectEventType), title, metadata (Json), actorName |
| **Model** | `models` | id, modelCode (unique), fullName, phone, whatsapp, email, photo, status |
| **ProjectModel** | `project_models` | id, projectId FK, modelId FK, videosCount, pricePerVideo, totalAmount, paymentStatus |
| **Quote** | `quotes` | id, quoteCode (unique), clientId FK, projectId FK?, subtotal, discount, tax, total |
| **QuoteItem** | `quote_items` | id, quoteId FK, description, quantity, unitPrice, total |
| **Invoice** | `invoices` | id, invoiceCode (unique), clientId, projectId?, quoteId? (unique), status, subtotal, total, paidAmount, remainingAmount |
| **InvoiceItem** | `invoice_items` | Similar to QuoteItem |
| **Payment** | `payments` | id, paymentCode (unique), invoiceId FK, amount, paymentMethod |
| **TeamMember** | `team_members` | id, employeeCode (unique), fullName, email (unique), phone, role (TeamRole), status |
| **ProjectAssignment** | `project_assignments` | id, projectId FK, teamMemberId FK, role |
| **Shoot** | `shoots` | id, shootCode (unique), projectId FK, title, date, startTime, endTime, status |
| **ShootAssignment** | `shoot_assignments` | id, shootId FK, teamMemberId FK?, modelId FK?, role |
| **Task** | `tasks` | id, taskCode (unique), projectId FK, shootId FK?, parentTaskId FK?, title, status, priority, assignedTo FK?, progress, order |
| **TaskChecklist** | `task_checklists` | id, taskId FK, title, completed |
| **TaskComment** | `task_comments` | id, taskId FK, authorId FK (TeamMember), content |
| **TaskAttachment** | `task_attachments` | id, taskId FK, fileName, storageKey, mime, size |
| **ActivityLog** | `activity_logs` | id, userId FK, type (ActivityType), entity, entityId, metadata (Json) |
| **Notification** | `notifications` | id, userId FK, type (NotificationType), title, message, entity, entityId, readAt |
| **ProjectFile** | `project_files` | id, projectId FK, fileType (ProjectFileType), storageKey, fileName, fileSize, mimeType |

**Token/Public Access Models**:
- `Client.token` — unique token per client
- `Project.token` — unique token per project (used at `/p/[token]`)
- `Delivery.slug` — unique slug (used at `/delivery/[slug]`)
- `Delivery.passwordHash` — optional password protection

### 4. Existing UI Components

**shadcn/ui Components** (`src/components/ui/` — 12 files):
`avatar`, `badge`, `button`, `card`, `dialog`, `dropdown-menu`, `input`, `label`, `separator`, `sheet`, `skeleton`, `tooltip`

**Review Studio Components** (`src/components/review/` — 11 files):
`comment-form.tsx`, `comment-item.tsx`, `comments-panel.tsx`, `phone-frame.tsx`, `review-animations.tsx`, `review-player.tsx`, `review-status-badge.tsx`, `review-studio.tsx`, `revision-dialog.tsx`, `safe-zones-overlay.tsx`, `social-overlays.tsx`

**Player Components** (`src/components/player/` — 5 files):
`gallery-player.tsx`, `photo-viewer.tsx`, `player-controls.tsx`, `video-player.tsx`, `watermarked-player.tsx`

**Delivery Components** (`src/components/delivery/` — 7 files):
`delivery-center.tsx`, `delivery-portal-access.tsx`, `delivery-portal.tsx`, `disabled-screen.tsx`, `expired-screen.tsx`, `password-screen.tsx`, `public-gallery.tsx`

**Layout Components** (`src/components/layout/` — 5 files):
`dashboard-shell.tsx`, `footer.tsx`, `header.tsx`, `mobile-nav.tsx`, `sidebar.tsx`

**Shared Components** (`src/components/shared/` — 8 files):
`action-button.tsx`, `confirm-dialog.tsx`, `empty-state.tsx`, `notification-menu.tsx`, `page-header.tsx`, `section-title.tsx`, `sidebar-item.tsx`, `user-menu.tsx`

**Domain-specific components** (7 directories):
`analytics/`, `calendar/`, `clients/`, `finance/`, `models/`, `projects/`, `tasks/`, `team/`, `uploads/`, `wizard/`, `workflow/`, `forms/`

### 5. Review Studio — Full Feature Inventory

The primary component is **`ReviewStudio`** in `src/components/review/review-studio.tsx` (~700 lines):

**Features implemented**:
- **Phone mockup**: `PhoneFrame` with titanium border gradient, Dynamic Island, speaker grill, glass reflections, gesture bar — 9:16 aspect ratio
- **Status bar**: `StatusBar` with live time, signal bars, WiFi icon, battery indicator
- **Social platform overlays**: `InstagramOverlay`, `TikTokOverlay`, `YouTubeOverlay`, `FacebookOverlay` — segmented control switcher
- **Safe zones overlay**: `SafeZoneOverlay` with red (platform UI), yellow (danger), green (safe) translucent overlays — configurable per platform (Instagram 10%/28%/12%, TikTok 6%/30%/14%, etc.)
- **Play/Pause**: Tap-to-toggle with play icon overlay when paused
- **Fullscreen**: Via `requestFullscreen()` API
- **Volume**: Mute/unmute toggle + volume slider
- **Playback speed**: Menu showing 0.5x, 1x, 1.25x, 1.5x, 2x
- **Seek bar**: With timeline markers (comments as dots) and hover tooltips
- **Comments system**: Full CRUD — create at timestamp, reply, resolve, archive, filter, search
- **Approval workflow**: Approve button → transitions to `APPROVED`; Revision button → opens `RevisionDialog` (transitions to `REVISION`)
- **Download**: For `FINAL_VIDEO` when workflow is `DELIVERED`/`COMPLETED`
- **Double-tap heart**: Animated heart at tap position
- **Swipe navigation**: `useSwipeNavigation` hook for vertical swipe between videos
- **Hold-to-speed**: `useHoldToSpeed` — hold video for 2x playback
- **Auto-hide UI**: `useAutoHideUI` — hides controls during playback
- **Screenshot mode**: Chromeless view for clean screenshots
- **Presentation mode**: Full-screen chromeless for presentations
- **Draft comments**: Auto-save via `useDraftComment` hook
- **Keyboard shortcuts**: Via `useReviewKeyboard` hook
- **Desktop timeline**: Separate TimelineBar under the phone on desktop
- **Video selector**: Bottom tab bar for switching between multiple videos
- **Comment form**: Floating inline form at timestamp with name/email fields

**ReviewPlayer** (`src/components/review/review-player.tsx`): A simpler alternative for the public delivery portal — standard video player + comments panel + approve button.

### 6. API Routes

| Route | Method | Auth | Purpose |
|-------|--------|------|---------|
| `/api/health` | GET | No | Health check |
| `/api/upload` | POST | Yes | File upload endpoint |
| `/api/webhooks` | POST | TBD | Webhook receiver |

### 7. Auth Pattern — Supabase Auth + Prisma

**Architecture**: Dual-source auth:
1. **Supabase Auth** manages credentials, sessions, and session cookies
2. **Prisma `User` table** stores profile (name, role, avatar) linked via `supabaseUid`

**Flow**:
- **Session reads** (`src/lib/auth/session.ts`): `getCurrentUser()` reads supabase session → looks up Prisma user by `supabaseUid` → returns `SessionUser` or null
- **Role guards**: `requireUser()`, `requireRole(...roles)` — throws `UnauthorizedError`/`ForbiddenError`
- **RBAC** (`src/lib/auth/rbac.ts`): `requireAuth()`, `requireAdmin()`, `requireManagement()` — used in Server Actions
- **Permissions** (`src/lib/auth/permissions.ts`): `requireOwner()`, `requireAdmin()`, `requireEditor()`, `requireMediaBuyer()`, `requirePhotographer()` — fine-grained guards

**Middleware (proxy.ts)**:
- Next.js 16 `proxy()` function (replaces middleware)
- Refreshes Supabase session
- Protects `/dashboard/*` routes → redirects to `/login`
- Redirects authenticated users away from `/login`
- Sets security headers: CSP, HSTS, X-Frame-Options, etc.
- Matcher: `/((?!_next/static|_next/image|favicon.ico|logo|icons|images).*)`

**Public/Token-based access** exists:
- `/delivery/[slug]` — No auth required. Optional password protection via `delivery.passwordHash`
- `/p/[token]` — Token-based access via `project.token`
- The `accessDeliveryAction` server action is public (no `getCurrentUser()` call)

**Auth actions** (`src/actions/auth/`): `login.ts`, `logout.ts`, `register.ts`, `update-profile.ts`
**Login flow**: `loginAction` → `authService.login()` → `supabase.auth.signInWithPassword()` → looks up Prisma user → returns `AuthenticatedUser`

**Session provider** (`src/providers/session-provider.tsx`): React Context with `user`, `status` (`loading`/`authenticated`/`unauthenticated`), `setUser`, `setStatus`

### 8. Storage Pattern — Cloudflare R2 (via AWS SDK) + Signed URLs

**Provider**: Cloudflare R2 (S3-compatible) — `src/lib/storage/cloudflare-r2.provider.ts`
- Uses `@aws-sdk/client-s3` and `@aws-sdk/s3-request-presigner`
- Lazy-initialized S3 client
- Operations: `upload`, `replace`, `delete`, `moveFile`, `copyFile`, `listFiles`, `exists`, `getMetadata`, `getSignedUrl`, `getDownloadUrl`, `getStreamingUrl`, `generateUploadUrl`

**Storage service** (`src/lib/storage/storage.service.ts`): Singleton facade — the only storage entry point

**Signed URL expiration**:
- Download: 1 hour (3600s)
- Streaming: 2 hours (7200s)
- Upload (presigned): 10 minutes (600s)

**Config** (`src/lib/storage/config.ts`): All credentials from `process.env` (R2_ACCOUNT_ID, R2_ACCESS_KEY_ID, R2_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY, R2_BUCKET_NAME, R2_ENDPOINT)

**Upload limits** (from `src/lib/constants`): MAX_FILE_SIZE, MAX_VIDEO_DURATION_SECONDS, ALLOWED_VIDEO_FORMATS, ALLOWED_MIME_TYPES

**Supabase is used for Auth ONLY, NOT for storage.** The env file confirms: `# Supabase (Auth only — NOT Storage)`

**Video model** stores: `storageKey`, `storageBucket`, `streamUrl`, `downloadUrl`, `thumbnailUrl` — signed URLs generated on access

### 9. Styling — Tailwind CSS v4 + shadcn/ui

**Tailwind config** (`tailwind.config.ts`):
- Content: `./src/**/*.{ts,tsx,mdx}`
- Fonts: Cairo (Arabic primary), Inter (Latin fallback)
- Container: centered, 1.5rem padding, max 1400px
- Custom breakpoints: `xs: 400px`, `3xl: 1920px`

- Lazy-initialized S3 client
- Operations: `upload`, `replace`, `delete`, `moveFile`, `copyFile`, `listFiles`, `exists`, `getMetadata`, `getSignedUrl`, `getDownloadUrl`, `getStreamingUrl`, `generateUploadUrl`

**Storage service** (`src/lib/storage/storage.service.ts`): Singleton facade — the only storage entry point

### 7. Auth Pattern — Supabase Auth + Prisma

**Architecture**: Dual-source auth:
1. **Supabase Auth** manages credentials, sessions, and session cookies
2. **Prisma `User` table** stores profile (name, role, avatar) linked via `supabaseUid`

**Flow**:
- **Session reads** (`src/lib/auth/session.ts`): `getCurrentUser()` reads supabase session → looks up Prisma user by `supabaseUid` → returns `SessionUser` or null
- **Role guards**: `requireUser()`, `requireRole(...roles)` — throws `UnauthorizedError`/`ForbiddenError`
- **RBAC** (`src/lib/auth/rbac.ts`): `requireAuth()`, `requireAdmin()`, `requireManagement()` — used in Server Actions
- **Permissions** (`src/lib/auth/permissions.ts`): `requireOwner()`, `requireAdmin()`, `requireEditor()`, `requireMediaBuyer()`, `requirePhotographer()` — fine-grained guards

**Middleware (proxy.ts)**:
- Next.js 16 `proxy()` function (replaces middleware)
- Refreshes Supabase session
- Protects `/dashboard/*` routes → redirects to `/login`
- Redirects authenticated users away from `/login`
- Sets security headers: CSP, HSTS, X-Frame-Options, etc.
- Matcher: `/((?!_next/static|_next/image|favicon.ico|logo|icons|images).*)`

**Public/Token-based access** exists:
- `/delivery/[slug]` — No auth required. Optional password protection via `delivery.passwordHash`
- `/p/[token]` — Token-based access via `project.token`
- The `accessDeliveryAction` server action is public (no `getCurrentUser()` call)

**Auth actions** (`src/actions/auth/`): `login.ts`, `logout.ts`, `register.ts`, `update-profile.ts`
**Login flow**: `loginAction` → `authService.login()` → `supabase.auth.signInWithPassword()` → looks up Prisma user → returns `AuthenticatedUser`

**Session provider** (`src/providers/session-provider.tsx`): React Context with `user`, `status` (`loading`/`authenticated`/`unauthenticated`), `setUser`, `setStatus`

### 8. Storage Pattern — Cloudflare R2 (via AWS SDK) + Signed URLs

**Provider**: Cloudflare R2 (S3-compatible) — `src/lib/storage/cloudflare-r2.provider.ts`
- Uses `@aws-sdk/client-s3` and `@aws-sdk/s3-request-presigner`
- Lazy-initialized S3 client
- Operations: `upload`, `replace`, `delete`, `moveFile`, `copyFile`, `listFiles`, `exists`, `getMetadata`, `getSignedUrl`, `getDownloadUrl`, `getStreamingUrl`, `generateUploadUrl`

**Storage service** (`src/lib/storage/storage.service.ts`): Singleton facade — the only storage entry point

**Signed URL expiration**:
- Download: 1 hour (3600s)
- Streaming: 2 hours (7200s)
- Upload (presigned): 10 minutes (600s)

**Config** (`src/lib/storage/config.ts`): All credentials from `process.env` (R2_ACCOUNT_ID, R2_ACCESS_KEY_ID, R2_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY, R2_BUCKET_NAME, R2_ENDPOINT)

**Upload limits** (from `src/lib/constants`): MAX_FILE_SIZE, MAX_VIDEO_DURATION_SECONDS, ALLOWED_VIDEO_FORMATS, ALLOWED_MIME_TYPES

**Supabase is used for Auth ONLY, NOT for storage.** The env file confirms: `# Supabase (Auth only — NOT Storage)`

**Video model** stores: `storageKey`, `storageBucket`, `streamUrl`, `downloadUrl`, `thumbnailUrl` — signed URLs generated on access

functionDeclarations.name: glob
functionDeclarations.description: Efficiently finds files matching specific glob patterns (e.g., `src/**/*.ts`, `**/*.md`), returning absolute paths sorted by modification time (newest first). Ideal for quickly locating files based on their name or path structure, especially in large codebases.

pattern.description: The glob pattern to match against (e.g., '**/*.py', 'docs/*.md').

path.description: Optional: The absolute path to the directory to search within. If omitted, searches the root directory.

case_sensitive.description: Optional: Whether the search should be case-sensitive. Defaults to false.

include_hidden.description: Optional: Whether to descend into dot-prefixed directories (e.g. '.git/', '.codely-cli/') and match dot-prefixed files. Defaults to false to mirror search content tools. Set to true to scan hidden paths explicitly.

maxResults.description: Optional: Maximum number of file paths to return (default: 100). Use to limit output for broad patterns. When the cap is hit the result is paginated; pass 'offset' set to the previous offset+maxResults to continue.

offset.description: Optional: Number of matching file paths to skip before returning results. Use this to continue after a truncated result's next offset.

functionDeclarations.name: list_directory
functionDeclarations.description: Lists the names of files and subdirectories directly within a specified directory path. Can optionally ignore entries matching provided glob patterns.

path.description: The absolute path to the directory to list (must be absolute, not relative)

ignore.description: List of glob patterns to ignore

functionDeclarations.name: read_file
functionDeclarations.description: Reads and returns the content of a specified file. By default reads up to 2000 lines starting from the beginning of the file. Files whose selected range exceeds 256 KB are truncated with an advisory; use 'offset' and 'limit' only if the file is too large to read at once — it's recommended to read the whole file by not providing these parameters. Handles text files. For images, PDFs, videos, audio or other multimedia files, this tool cannot present their contents to the model — use the 'analyze_multimedia' tool instead.

absolute_path.description: The absolute path to the file to read (e.g., '/home/user/project/file.txt'). Relative paths are not supported. You must provide an absolute path.

offset.description: Optional: For text files, the 0-based line number to start reading from. Only provide if the file is too large to read at once. Note: search_file_content reports matches as 1-based `Lxx` line numbers; to jump from a hit at `L42` here, subtract 1 — pass `offset: 41` (i.e. `offset = lineNumber - 1`).

limit.description: Optional: For text files, the maximum number of lines to read. Only provide if the file is too large to read at once.

functionDeclarations.name: run_shell_command
functionDeclarations.description:
This tool executes a given shell command using the best available Windows shell (PowerShell Core, Windows PowerShell, or Command Prompt).

IMPORTANT:
- This tool is for terminal operations (e.g., git / npm / docker / build & test commands).
- DO NOT use this tool for file operations like reading, writing, editing, or searching files. Prefer the dedicated file tools for those actions.
- Quote any file path that contains spaces with double quotes, e.g. `cd "/path with spaces"` or `python "/path with spaces/script.py"`.
- If a command would produce very large output, prefer adding flags/filters (narrow paths, limit counts, request specific fields, machine-readable formats like JSON) to keep output small and unambiguous.
- Very large outputs may be returned as the most recent tail of the output. Earlier content may be truncated from the beginning. If the tool reports a "Full output saved to: <path>", that file contains the full command output. Use the exact path returned by the tool, then prefer searching that file first (grep/rg or platform equivalents) for specific ERROR/WARN/FAIL/SUCCESS markers, then reading small slices (with `read_file` using `offset`/`limit`) or tailing the log file instead of re-running expensive commands.
- Do not append truncating pipelines such as `| tail`, `| Select-Object -Last`, or similar to an expensive first run just to suppress output. Those discard earlier context and force a re-run if you later need omitted lines. Let the tool/log capture the full output, then inspect the saved log file.
- When issuing multiple shell commands in one response, parallelize only when they are truly independent and do not compete for the same files, directories, logs, locks, caches, git state, or ports. If one command depends on another or shared state may race, run them sequentially.

The following information is returned:
- Command: Executed command.
- Directory: Directory where command was executed, or `(root)`.
- Output: Combined command output (stdout/stderr). Can be `(empty)` or an excerpt if very large.
- Error: Error or `(none)` if no error was reported for the subprocess.
- Exit Code: Exit code or `(none)` if terminated by signal.
- Signal: Signal number or `(none)` if no signal was received.

command.description: Exact command to execute using the best available Windows shell (automatically selected from PowerShell Core, Windows PowerShell, or Command Prompt). Quote any file paths containing spaces with double quotes.

description.description: Brief description of the command for the user. Be specific and concise. Ideally a single sentence. Can be up to 3 sentences for clarity. No line breaks.

directory.description: (OPTIONAL) The absolute path of the directory to run the command in. If not provided, the project root directory is used. Must be a directory within the workspace and must already exist.

functionDeclarations.name: search_file_content
functionDeclarations.description: Searches for a regular expression pattern within the content of files in a specified directory, a specific file, or the current working directory. The search backend is ripgrep, so regex patterns use ripgrep/Rust regex syntax, not JavaScript RegExp syntax. If you need to search literal text that contains regex metacharacters (for example $, {, [, (, or \), set fixed_strings: true or -F: true instead of escaping by hand. Can filter files by a glob pattern when searching directories. By default, returns matching lines with file paths and line numbers; set output_mode to "files_with_matches" for just file paths or "count" for per-file match totals. Each matched line is truncated at 500 characters with a "[line truncated from N chars]" suffix to keep minified or base64 content from blowing up context.

pattern.description: The regular expression pattern to search for within file contents. This is interpreted by ripgrep/Rust regex, not JavaScript RegExp; avoid JS-only features such as lookbehind and backreferences. Regex syntax errors are returned by ripgrep. For literal text, especially strings containing regex metacharacters like $, {, [, (, or \, prefer fixed_strings: true or -F: true. When passing JSON args, escape only for JSON once: to match literal ${, use pattern "\\$\\{" (the regex engine sees \$\{), not "\\\\$\\\\{".

path.description: Optional: A path relative to the workspace root, or an absolute path to a directory or file to search within. If a file path is provided, searches only within that file. If a directory path is provided, searches all files in that directory. If omitted, searches the current working directory.

glob.description: Optional: A glob pattern to filter which files are searched (e.g., '*.js', '*.{ts,tsx}', 'src/**'). Single string only — combine multiple patterns via whitespace/comma separation (e.g. '*.js *.ts', '*.js,*.ts') or brace expansion ('*.{ts,tsx}'). Only applicable when searching directories, ignored for file searches. If omitted, searches all files (respecting potential global ignores).

output_mode.description: Optional: Output format. 'content' (default) returns matching lines with line numbers and optional context; 'files_with_matches' returns only the list of matching file paths; 'count' returns per-file match counts.

maxResults.description: Optional: Maximum number of matches to return to prevent context overflow (default: 250, max: 1000). Values above 1000 are clamped automatically. Use lower values for broad searches, higher for specific searches. Pass 0 for unlimited.

head_limit.description: Optional: Claude Code aligned alias for 'maxResults'. When both are provided, head_limit wins. Pass 0 for unlimited (use sparingly — large result sets waste context).

offset.description: Optional: Number of matches to skip before returning results. Use this to continue after a truncated result's next offset.

-A.description: Optional: Number of context lines to show after each match. Only applies to 'content' output_mode. Alias for ripgrep's -A.

-B.description: Optional: Number of context lines to show before each match. Only applies to 'content' output_mode. Alias for ripgrep's -B.

-C.description: Optional: Number of context lines to show before and after each match. Only applies to 'content' output_mode. Alias for ripgrep's -C.

context.description: Optional: Number of context lines to show before and after each match. Only applies to 'content' output_mode. Alias for -C.

-n.description: Optional: Whether to include line numbers in 'content' output. Defaults to true; set to false to omit them.

-i.description: Optional: Case-insensitive matching. Defaults to false (case-sensitive), matching ripgrep behavior.

fixed_strings.description: Optional: Treat the pattern as a literal string instead of a regular expression. Alias for -F.

-F.description: Optional: Treat the pattern as a literal string instead of a regular expression. Alias for fixed_strings.

type.description: Optional: ripgrep file type filter (rg --type). Common types: js, py, rust, go, java, etc. Accepts any alias from `rg --type-list`; the value is forwarded verbatim to ripgrep.

multiline.description: Optional: Enable multiline matching where patterns may span newlines (passes -U and --multiline-dotall to ripgrep). Only meaningful for 'content' output_mode. ^/$ anchors keep line boundaries behavior for portability across search backends.

include_hidden.description: Optional: Whether to descend into dot-prefixed directories (e.g. '.git/', '.codely-cli/') and match dot-prefixed files. Defaults to false to mirror ripgrep's default hidden-skip behaviour. Set to true to scan hidden paths explicitly (passes --hidden to ripgrep).

functionDeclarations.name: complete_task
functionDeclarations.description:
A non-empty response with no tool calls is treated as a successful final result.
Use complete_task when you need to report partial, blocked, or failure status, or when explicit next steps are important.

This tool must be called ALONE in its turn. Do not call any other tools in the same turn. If you still need another tool, do not call this yet.

Your result MUST be an informative handoff for the caller (main agent), not a one-line summary.

Requirements for result:
- Use 2+ short paragraphs or a short markdown sectioned report
- Include what you did / found and the concrete evidence that supports it
- Include file paths, errors, command outcomes, or other artifacts when relevant
- Do NOT return only a title or a single-line summary
- Keep it concise, but detailed enough that the main agent can act without reopening your full trace
- Choose a status that matches the outcome: use 'success' for fully completed work, 'partial' for meaningful progress with straightforward continuation, 'blocked' for external blockers that you cannot resolve autonomously, and 'failure' for invalid or unrecoverable paths
